items.hook: Brenda Fricker, Oscar winner for My Left Foot, dies aged 81
items.body:
The acclaimed Irish actor started her career in Coronation Street and Casualty before a string of high-profile Hollywood roles

Brenda Fricker, who became the first female Irish Oscar winner for acting with My Left Foot, has died ag

items.hook: Psychotropic alkylamines are alkylamines that share the critical property of not containing an aromatic nucleus, but are still biologically active.
items.body: While many of these molecules are stimulants, others are antiviral, have competitive NMDA antagonist activity, or are nicotinic receptor antagonists. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Courier was a prototype concept by Microsoft for a dual-touchscreen tablet.
items.body: The device was conceived as being a digital notebook, consisting of two 7-inch touchscreens hinged together like a book, and running a custom operating system built primarily around handwriting input and a notebook-like journal for storing notes, images, and c

items.hook: It was destroyed by the British in 1702 CE, and was rebuilt later by the French in 1792.
items.body: The Pointe aux Canons Battery was a historic fort located on the island of Saint Pierre in the French territorial collectivity of Saint Pierre and Miquelon. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Huyan Zan (呼延贊) was a Chinese military general in the early years of the Northern Song dynasty.
items.body: He participated in the Northern Song's conquest of the Later Shu in 964-965 and the Northern Han in 979. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Ballot Act 1872 in the United Kingdom introduced the requirement that parliamentary and local government elections be held by secret ballot.
items.body: The Ballot Act 1872 was an act of the Parliament of the United Kingdom that introduced the requirement for parliamentary and local government elections in the United Kingdom to be held by secret ballot. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: What we’re reading: writers and readers on the books they enjoyed in June
items.body:
Candice Carty-Williams, Patrick Freyne and Guardian readers discuss the titles they have read over the last month. Join the conversation in the comments

I just finished reading Wimmy Road Boyz by Sufiyaan Salam. I

items.hook: In January 2021, it had a population of 1,919,745.
items.body: Alsace is a cultural region and a territorial collectivity in the Grand Est administrative region of northeastern France, on the west bank of the upper Rhine, next to Germany and Switzerland. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Using AI for creative pursuits? Moderation is key
items.body: An experiment showed AI users had the most creative ideas when they used it in moderation – not too much and not too little. Columnist David Robson puts the finding to the test, and explores what we lose when we over-rely on AI

items.hook: American Civil War: Second Battle of Fort Wagner: One of the first formal African American military units, the 54th Massachusetts Volunteer Infantry, supported by several white regiments, attempts an unsuccessful assault on Confederate-held Battery Wagner.
items.body: The American Civil War was a civil war in the United States between the Union and the Confederacy, which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Coronation of Emperor Pedro II of Brazil.
items.body: Dom Pedro II, known as "the Magnanimous", was the second and final emperor of the Empire of Brazil. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Red Mouth by Sheila Armstrong review – profound exploration of Ireland’s deep time
items.body:
Sinister bogland discoveries haunt the intersecting lives of four characters in this meditative, exquisitely written tale

Almost 14% of Ireland is bog: vast swathes of moss-carpeted land, below which layers of ancient history have

items.hook: A picture of Les Horribles Cernettes was taken, which became the first ever photo posted to the World Wide Web.
items.body: Les Horribles Cernettes was an all-female parody pop group, self-labelled "the one and only High Energy Rock Band", which was founded by employees of CERN and performed at CERN and other HEP-related events. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Storms provoke severe flooding on the Saguenay River, beginning one of Quebec's costliest natural disasters ever.
items.body: The Saguenay flood was a series of flash floods on July 19 and 20, 1996 that hit the Saguenay–Lac-Saint-Jean region of Quebec, Canada. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.body: Image: Mars Express image

items.hook: Kyrkliga Folkpartiet was a minor pro-fascist party in Sweden founded in 1930.
items.body: The party was formed and led by Ivar Rhedin, a priest in the Church of Sweden. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: All 135 seats in the Parliament were up for election.
items.body: A regional election was held in Catalonia on 20 March 1980 to elect the 1st Parliament of the autonomous community. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Techno Source is a handheld electronic game and TV game company selling electronic toys, games and learning aids.
items.body: Based in Hong Kong with an office in New York City, it is a privately owned company founded in 2000 by Wayne Nathan and Rich Migatz. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Bayeux Tapestry in 'wonderful state' after journey to London, French minister says
items.body: The French culture minister has told the BBC that the Bayeux Tapestry is in "a wonderful state".

items.hook: Vladimir Tismăneanu is a Romanian American political scientist, political analyst, sociologist, and professor at the University of Maryland, College Park.
items.body: A specialist in political systems and comparative politics, he is director of the University of Maryland's Center for the Study of Post-Communist Societies, having served as chairman of the editorial committee (2004–2008) and editor (1998–2004) of the East Eur

items.hook: ‘Colony’ director Yeon Sang-ho talks sequel plans through novel, video game
items.body: EXCLUSIVE: The film has proved a box office hit in South Korea and is set for a US release on August 28.

items.hook: Rwandan genocide: The Rwandan Patriotic Front takes control of Gisenyi and north western Rwanda, forcing the interim government into Zaire and ending the genocide.
items.body: The Rwandan genocide, also known as the Tutsi genocide, occurred from 7 April to 19 July 1994 during the Rwandan Civil War. One of those things you can't un-know.
